text: VO2max is determined by an integrated system of O2 transport conductances in the lungs, circulation, and skeletal muscle, with all components interacting to define the final value.
The more athletic an individual is, the more sensitive their VO2max becomes to O2 transport conductances in the lungs, circulation, and skeletal muscle.
Diffusive O2 conductance in skeletal muscle is a particularly important component in determining VO2max.
Correcting central cardiovascular defects in O2 transport alone may fail to restore exercise capacity if peripheral muscle diffusive conductance remains impaired.
VO2max limitations shift from central (cardiac output, blood oxygen carrying capacity) to peripheral (muscle O2 diffusion) factors as fitness level increases.
